reason: The spec governs the whole vocabulary/dispatch hub — the dispatch switch is top-level code and the body covers grammar, signposts, help, and internal boundary equally across the file.
runMigrate is the one-shot migrator the body narrates end-to-end: preconditions, the three rewrite tiers, staged output, and post-checks.

The doctor function produces the per-layer diagnosis report (preconditions/contract/hooks/trust/git-hooks/backend/settings) that is the body's core promise; the flag routes are thin wrappers around it.
The contract IS the conflict-marker scan over the three source trees that drives the exit-75 guard; conflicted is that scan (the print/exit is top-level code attached to it).
assertSessionRuntime is the gate the body promises: probe the tmux primitive, print the actionable block, exit 69 before any cryptic downstream failure.
prepack.mjs is a top-level build script (its only named units are path constants); the packaging contract lives in the script as a whole plus package.json, not in any one unit.
commitSurgery implements the staged-index repair (block-strip in place, husk eviction, never delete tracked) that is the body's central mechanism.

The body's promises map one-to-one onto distinct units — SHIM (clean/smudge invariant), plantContractFilter, settleIndexStat (phantom-M), removeContractFilter (ordered unplant) — with no single dominant unit.
This file implements only the spec's third transport (copy-then-hide of spexcode.local.json), and seedWorktreeHostState is its entry, calling hideSeededFromGit.
specInit is the whole bootstrap the body narrates: git precondition, seed tree copy, hooks, starter spexcode.json, harness validation, and the truthful success message.
uninstall is the identity-stamped total backout the body describes; the helper sweeps are subroutines it drives.
The body names getBoard as the one seam every graph read goes through, carrying single-flight, scoped splice/rebuild, cache-until-change, and the settlement watchdog.
The body names unitize/tagOf/diffUnits/applyDelta/boardFromUnits as one algebra with equal weight; the whole pure module is the contract.
buildBoard is the assembly where every lean cut (dropped body/parts, latest-per-scenario evals, slim scenarios) is applied to the wire payload.
The contract spans fireChanged scoping, the watcher/poller/patrol sources, rebuildAndBroadcast, and the boardStream route about equally — the file is the channel.
The spec governs each topic page's content (SETUP/SPEC/EVAL/SETTINGS/FOOTPRINT const texts) as much as the tiny guideText dispatcher; anchoring the dispatcher would blind drift to page edits.
The body covers the whole store mechanism — format, parse/serialize, lock, trunk commit, all write verbs, remarks, both nudges — dozens of units with equal weight.
staleAxes is the 'one computation fed at the call sites' that takes the remark track explicitly and fires the remark axis every surface reads (remarkStale is its narrow inner predicate).

The body gives equal weight to the merged read (mergedIssues/findIssue), the forge translation (fromForge), and the store-routed write verbs (createIssue/replyIssue/closeIssue/promote) — no single unit dominates.
The node's core claim — '@ just auto-sends a prompt', @new spawn with parent rule, the drain guard — all live in dispatchMentions, the dispatch verb the grammar exists to feed.
startGateway IS the gateway the body narrates — TLS termination, the opt-in login gate, static dist, /api and WebSocket proxying are wired there.
The governed file is the test suite proving withSenderHint (which lives in sessions.ts); its only units are trivial fixtures, so the whole test file carries the proof.
The governed file is a test whose units (mk/ID/waitFor) are helpers; the contract is exercised by the test file as a whole, not one unit.
The contract is the whole adapter boundary — the Harness interface is typeOnly, and claudeHarness/codexHarness plus their many implementation functions share the body's weight.
The body names its ownership as the vocabulary + validation (resolveHarnessTargets) — both fail-loud invariants live in this function.
The node IS the emitter — emitPlugin materializes the whole self-contained bundle the body narrates; the other units are its pieces.
The body's whole contract (git-native anchors, per-harness artifacts, the forgetting law's erase-then-assert) is the materialize pass itself.
The only unit in hooks.ts and the spec's compiler part — it flattens surface:hook nodes into the persistent manifest the dispatcher reads.
The spec narrates the entire bridge equally — byte parsing, resize convergence, size vote, frame reconstruction, wheel routing, supervisor — no single unit dominates.
The governed file is the width test suite as a whole; its only top-level unit is a fixture helper, not the contract.
The contract spans the routing ladder, the write guard, selector resolution, and a dozen thin client verbs equally — the file is the module.
The verb itself — selector resolve, detach hint, foreground attach, TTY/locality/offline guards — is what the body promises.
The governed wire-shape test file carries the contract as a whole; its only unit is a record fixture helper.
The governed selectors test file's units are all fixtures (mk, a, b, c, board); the contract is the suite, not one unit.
The spec explicitly owns sessions.ts as the whole shared substrate monolith — anchoring one unit would contradict its foundation-owner intent.
The body's one surface claim is buildBoard assembling tree + overlay + sessions + identity served at /api/graph and spex graph --json.

The raw source frames the whole node around one gate question — did any window commit intersect the anchored unit's lines — and anchorHitCommits is the function that composes windowCommits, per-commit unitsAt extraction, and hunk∩range into that verdict.

main enumerates exactly the surfaces the spec declares dead (source roots, shell hooks, file/dir basenames, .spec node dir names) and applies the prose exemptions — the scan-scope contract lives here.

driftFor is the pure ancestry lookup the body promises — a commit is drift iff not reachable from the version, netted against the Spec-OK ack cover — and is the one rule every consumer of the signal reads.
The body explicitly names mintIds as THE one exported mint that guarantees the single-token URL-safe id invariant every resolve site relies on.
The body names resolveLayout() as the one seam's answer — main location, source-of-truth branch, governed-record enumeration, and the board's ops overlay all flow from it.
specLint (lines 126-357) implements the entire rule registry the body enumerates — every error and warn tier the contract promises fires from this one function.
loadSpecs is the loader/aggregator that derives a node's whole observable state (version, drift, session, status) from git at read time — the derive-not-store promise the body makes.
The body declares 'all behind one entrypoint rankDocs(query, docs)' — the shared scoring contract both callers depend on funnels through it.
The body names searchSpecs as the retriever carrying the locked output contract; the other units are its helpers.
index.ts is the whole HTTP entrypoint — the body narrates the full route surface and serve topology, and the routes are top-level wiring, not any single unit (the only units are trivial consts).
renderTree carries the node's main narrated contract — the indented tree, badges, colour palette, and the base-state labelling law with its NO_COLOR degradation.
guardWorktree is the existence-vs-details guard the body centers on: branch on the directory, omit only genuine removal, serve a degraded row on a transient detail failure.
The contract spans both units equally — projectApiUrl carries the walk-up resolution and the target line carries the three-source precedence — in a tiny file that is the module.
App is the entry the body narrates for this file: it boots the shared data layer, owns the fail-loud boot, the push-first board paths, and picks the face by viewport width.
The contract is split across the global stack, the module-scope capture listener (not a listed unit), and useEscLayer — the whole module is the mechanism, no single unit holds it.
EvalsGroup is the feed component owning the kind filter, mustShow widen, sticky head, and visible-row reporting the body promises.
EventDetail (89-586) IS the pane the spec narrates — header/stage/rail workspace, A/B strip, clip player, remark composer — dwarfing the helper units.
The three units map one-to-one onto the spec's parts (registry, per-render binding, preset fill) with no dominant one; the small file is the module.

EvalsPage carries most of the contract — the top-level route, the canonical address deep-link/echo, the board data path, and it composes the shared shell — while EvalMasterDetail holds only the geometry slice.
FoldToggle is the shared button the three homes render — the affordance with the fold/unfold title/aria contract — and it wraps the icon in its render.
FocusPanel is the panel component the body describes end-to-end (head, scenarios join, issues list, lite-corpus prose fill).
returnFocus implements the core promise — return-on-close to ticket else sink, never body, deferred one frame — the ticket recorder being unanchorable module scope.
The spec's contract for keymap.js IS the declarative action table ({id, keys, rebind, desc}) that the handler and help legend both project from — the data is the contract.
GraphStats is the strip component owning the three clusters, chip walks, and ScoreBadge reuse; wider than summarize alone and named as the node's only owned source.
The body narrates the two-row node tile — status dot, op glyphs, recency, badges, collapsed ▸N tab — all rendered by the SpecNode component.
The file's single unit is the custom right-click menu the spec describes end to end.

The body claims the popup shell plus the spec/history/issues/edit panes equally — SpecPane, ChronoPane, HistoryPane, IssuesPane, EditPane, panesFor and NodeView all carry contract clauses, so no single unit dominates.
The contract is explicitly 'one file, one contract': the ICONS registry, Icon's stroke/fail-loud rules, and IconButton's label requirement are co-equal pillars, so the module as a whole is governed.
The body says outright 'this node owns that shared helper' — the liveSession aliveness judgment both surfaces must share.
IssuesPage is the top-level master-detail page the body narrates (list column, filter bars, chips, selection-is-detail); the detail and New form are sub-surfaces it hosts.
MobileApp is the phone face's shell — the viewport switch, the two planes (Specs/Sessions), and the lazy-chunk entry the body promises.
The one-mechanism-for-every-pane hook is the file's single unit and the entire contract (drag, clamp, persist).
The governed file (selfSummary.test.ts) exposes no top-level units to anchor.
The bulk-close select bar and its confirm are named by the spec as this node's own surface, and it is the file's single unit.
The right-click menu with rename/close/reset-order is the file's single unit and the spec's whole gesture surface.
SessionInterface (lines 70-906) is the routed console page the body describes — list, tabs, terminal, inputs, type mode — dwarfing the small helper units.
The upload sink's core promise — collision-proof safe name in the shared /tmp dir, absolute path returned for splicing — lives in saveUpload.
The entire reconnect state machine (backoff, stable reset, intentional-close suppression, health states) is this one factory.
Settings is the routed settings-page component that hosts the language, shortcuts, and theme sections the body promises.
The body names the constructors, addressHash, and navigateAddress as equal halves of one closed vocabulary — the whole small file is the contract.
SideBar is the rail component the body narrates; ENTRIES and RailButton are its internals.
The body says the singleton TooltipLayer is the whole system; every promised behavior lives in it.
No units exist in main.jsx and the spec governs the whole package, not one unit.
The body normatively pins the hash definition to scenarios.ts scenarioHash as 'the one definition both filing seams and freshness read'.
The body enumerates readingScore, scenarioStates, aggregateState, ScenarioCount, TagChips, and ScoreBadge as co-equal pieces of one vocabulary — no single unit dominates.
The read engine's contract — readings joined with expected, freshness, and codeDrift into the timeline — is evalTimeline.
blobGet is the hash-in-bytes-out verb: local cache first, backend fallback, fail loud naming both paths.
blobPut is the bare transport half — put bytes, print the content hash, file nothing.
The contract (roster wiring, scoring loop, table, baseline gate) is spread across top-level script code and several small units — no single unit dominates the benchmark script.
The file's one behavior-bearing function implements the whole ok write: latest-reading anchoring, refusal on unmeasured, idempotent duplicate, sidecar append.
The spec narrates three co-equal faces of the module — buildExportModel, renderExportHtml, and buildSessionEvals (the lean tab model) — so the file as a whole is the contract.
The format contract is carried equally by validateTimeline (LOUD validation), normalizeTimeline (lossless v1 back-compat), and stepAt (the lookup); the module is the format.
The resident-cache contract — always-instant view, TTL-backed background reconcile, silent swallow on no-forge — is implemented in refreshIfStale.
The spec's state/delta/view and reconcile-is-truth invariants are exactly the ForgeCache class; whole class preferred over any one method.
The spec promises two verbs (links and --pending) plus the shared readForge factoring in equal measure; the file is the CLI surface, no single unit dominates.
The node's core promise is the resolution ladder (config → origin hostname → default) with brief memoization, which is resolveForgeHost itself.
The driver object carries the whole contract — list/since-window/write verbs, iid mapping, state normalization, fail-loud — the const is the behavior, not a data table.
The spec's inversion contract (three link ways, longest-match branch, marker-wins de-dup, only-linked-nodes) is resolveLinks; the helpers only serve it.
The single output the spec promises — open+flagged issues narrowed per resolved node into NodeEvalPending[] — is resolveEvalPending.
Every unit in port.ts is typeOnly (the port's shapes and the ForgeDriver interface), and typeOnly anchors are disallowed — the file-as-contract is the right scope.
